items.title: [负面] I really want to like this game. I love lovecrafti...
items.summary: I really want to like this game. I love lovecraftian horror, love card games and even gacha. However, the translation is simply all over the place. That is such an obvious issue, especially when you deal with esoteric knowledge and materials. For a VN-based game, it's just too hard to follow the story line.

items.title:
[正面] GAMEPLAY
Morimens has a roguelike card gameplay at...
items.summary:
GAMEPLAY
Morimens has a roguelike card gameplay at its core and that's what makes it so interesting to me.

If you have played games like Slay the Spire then you have a good idea to how the gameplay is going to be, but it has some differences. The deckbuilding part is where the gacha is at, each character you get have a unique set of cards and to clear stages you must bring a team of characters. Said characters also belong to one of four unique factions, each faction has a special mechanic in battle and leads to very different playstyles even if 2 characters have similar cards available.

Being a gacha, the grind is inevitable but most resource stages that you'll have to grind can be cleared once manually and be finished again with a single button which is a big win.


If you're competitive there's a ranking board for a daily challenge and also a PVP mode, both with daily rewards. The PVP mode is completely divorced from the main gameplay loop and doesn't require leveling your cards to play it. It works like a TCG, as long as you have the card in your collection you can use it to its full extent.

F2P FRIENDLY GACHA
The game is very generous with free pulls. At the time of this review I'm still F2P and am a little over 3 months in. With that said I have most characters available in the standard banner and a few from limited banners while still maintaining a good amount of free pulls for future banners.

STORY
The story is very eldritch horror themed and loosely based of chtulhu. Even during moments of light heartness it manages to keep the dark themes lurking around that will keep you invested. With that being said, the game is machine translated (for the most part) which can impact a bit in the reading. The devs are working on proper translations.

The artstyle and soundtrack are also amazing and matches with the vibe of the game perfectly

TLDR
In short, Morimens is a F2P friendly gacha game that is unique because of:
A. Eldritch horror narrative
B. Card-based roguelike gameplay (and a bonus of TCG-like PVP)
If you have an interest in either of these, I will recommed you to check it out

The only big CON is the fact that it is machine translated but the devs are slowly working on updating to a proper translation

items.title: [正面] Як для гачі, гра дуже цікава з на диво динамічним ...
items.summary:
Як для гачі, гра дуже цікава з на диво динамічним геймплеєм.
Непоганий сюжет, гарні дизайни персонажів, ними цікаво грати і придумувати різні стратегії.
Ресурси фармити неважко, качати персонажів в принципі легко (принаймні до 60лвл). 
Контенту багато і навіть після закінчення денної "енергії" є багато що робити. 
Щодо гачі. Це, здається, найприємніша гача з усіх можливих. Персонажі падають на 25-30 крутці, копії вибивати легко, особливо до 50 рівня акаунту, коли є багато контенту для зачищення.
В цілому в цю гру дуже приємно та комфортно грати, аж не віриться, що це гача.
Мінуси. Ну, їх небагато? 
Переклад іноді не дуже, є деякі помилки (англ. мова), немає англ. озвучки, але причина, мабуть, в бюджеті, тому мінус сумнівний. 
Єдиний мінус, який можна прям яскраво виділити, так це пінг. Господибожемій, пінг в середньому 300, іноді до 2к підскакує. В жодній грі ще такого не було. Заважає прям не дуже сильно, але іноді око сіпається від провисань. 

А так гру рекомендую тим, кому подобаються "карткові гачі" типу реверсу, наприклад. Після популярних гач, назви яких не буду згадувати, бо і так зрозуміло, ця гра - ковток свіжого повітря.

items.title: [正面] Es gatcha si pero el gameplay es realmente bueno y...
items.summary: Es gatcha si pero el gameplay es realmente bueno y no parece muy p2w TODAVIA. Tiene cosas que mejorar en el apartado feeling, los clics son medio feos en la pantalla, la interfaz no es del todo buena pero son detalles. Solido recomendado en su estado actual.

items.title: [正面] Fun, gacha investment is pretty low and it is very...
items.summary:
Fun, gacha investment is pretty low and it is very easy to pool all your resources into one character and keep winning. very f2p friendly (i have thought about spending but not yet, i do want to support this game tho so I wouldn't have any regrets doing it)

edit after 200 hours:
the endgame is challengine but incredibly satisfying. The recent boon of QoL updates and character buffing makes now one of the best times to join in as they celebrate and give free stuffs.
Basically, as a deckbuilding rougelike, you get 4 characters that have different specialties and path along fighting battles to buff cards, get passive buffs, or gain extra for whatever playstyle you're specializing in. 
The actual content and specializations are fun and enticing, with passive Dot, infant cannabilsm (my favorite), and turn skipping in turn based combat.

In terms of gacha, you are given a low floor in terms of getting a character and their weapon, and a ridiculous ceiling that is basically only possible for spenders. They do help longer term gameplay by allowing you to f2p buy extra copies of characters and weapons you already own (if you still want them to grow even after their banner ends), basically every gacha-esque rewards like dailies, weekly quests/battle pass, and permanent awards have an extra bonus if you spend.
In terms of "variety", there are actual men, and husbandos, and the balance of Male to Females are balanced with hotties all around with the cool lovecraftian theming or body horror.
If you are struggling in farming and grinding, there is the co-op borrowing a stranger's broken character, which I am glad to have, and glad to finally have a team strong enough to not need it in my main team.
Also you do want to build multiple teams, as the endgame content wants 5 fully built teams (for 20 characters and 40 weapons, no repeats allowed), something I haven't been doing because I've been having too much fun with my current one.

Overall, lovely game, low floor with ridiculous ceiling, very satisfying battles where I think out of my mind and blame my lack skill more than rng (despite it being a card game), music is nice and thematic.
